name: Los Embalses de Tablillas, Montoro I y Montoro II – circular desde Puertollano
region_intro: Mountain biking around Puertollano offers diverse natural landscapes, including volcanic terrain, river valleys, and mountain ranges. The region is characterized by an extensive network of trails that cater to various skill levels, from beginner-friendly paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. Riders can explore areas shaped by volcanic activity, such as the Campo de Calatrava, alongside scenic pastures and historical mining landscapes. The varied topography provides a range of experiences for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Puertollano The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Embalses de Tablillas, Montoro I y Montoro II – circular desde Puertollano, a 37.4 miles (60.2 km) difficult route that takes about 4 hours 34 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain around local reservoirs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puertollano a Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular, a moderate 17.5 miles (28.1 km) path. This route connects towns through varied terrain, offering a mix of landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Mestanza Village – Puertollano loop from Puertollano, a 27.4 miles (44.0 km) trail leading through the local countryside and villages, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Mountain biking around Puertollano is defined by volcanic landscapes, river valleys, and historical mining areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to demanding climbs. The routes in Puertollano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Puertollano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Puertollano?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails around Puertollano, catering to various skill levels. The region offers a diverse network of paths, from easy rides to challenging routes with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Puertollano?
A: Mountain biking in Puertollano features diverse terrain, including unique volcanic landscapes from the Campo de Calatrava, scenic river valleys, and historical mining areas. You'll encounter dry, hard, and winding sections, often with notable elevation changes, providing a varied riding experience.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Puertollano?
A: Yes, Puertollano offers several beginner-friendly mountain bike trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042269067/puertollano-a-argamasilla-de-calatrava-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertollano a Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that connects towns through varied terrain, suitable for those looking for a less demanding ride. The Vía Verde del Carbón y la Plata is also an easy, flat option following an old railway line.

Q: Where can I find more challenging mountain bike routes near Puertollano?
A: For more challenging rides, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040478122/los-embalses-de-tablillas-montoro-i-y-montoro-ii-circular-desde-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Embalses de Tablillas, Montoro I y Montoro II – circular desde Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 60.2 km route with significant elevation gain around local reservoirs. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of the Virgin of Carmen – Parque del Botijo loop from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, also rated difficult.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Puertollano?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Puertollano are circular. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040478122/los-embalses-de-tablillas-montoro-i-y-montoro-ii-circular-desde-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Embalses de Tablillas, Montoro I y Montoro II – circular desde Puertollano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042269067/puertollano-a-argamasilla-de-calatrava-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertollano a Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31899551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mestanza Village – Puertollano loop from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic circular experience through the countryside.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Puertollano?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore the unique volcanic landscapes of the Campo de Calatrava, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;. Routes also pass through the scenic Valle de Alcudia and the Puertollano mountain range, offering views of reservoirs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5632567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano del Montoro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible by mountain bike in the area?
A: Yes, the region has historical significance. You can find routes that lead to or offer views of historical landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Calatrava la Nueva and Sacred Convent&lt;/a&gt;. The Vía Verde del Carbón y la Plata also traverses historical mining landscapes, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Puertollano?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Puertollano is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters can bring cooler, potentially wet conditions, though the dry terrain often allows for year-round riding with appropriate preparation.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Puertollano?
A: The mountain bike trails around Puertollano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from volcanic terrain to river valleys, and the extensive network of trails that cater to all skill levels.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Absolutely. Several routes in the area incorporate reservoirs and lakes into their paths. A prime example is the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040478122/los-embalses-de-tablillas-montoro-i-y-montoro-ii-circular-desde-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Embalses de Tablillas, Montoro I y Montoro II – circular desde Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views around these water bodies. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5632567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano del Montoro&lt;/a&gt; in the region.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Puertollano?
A: Yes, the varied topography of Puertollano offers several opportunities for panoramic views. While not directly on a mountain bike trail, the Monument to the Miner on Cerro de Santa Ana provides excellent views of the city and surroundings. Some routes also offer scenic overlooks of the Valle de Alcudia and the Puertollano mountain range.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Puertollano?
A: For families, the Vía Verde del Carbón y la Plata is an excellent choice. This easy, 11.8 km path follows an old railway line, making it relatively flat and safe for riders of all ages. It offers a pleasant ride through mining landscapes and the Ojailén and Alcudia valleys.
tips:
Located at the foot of the Monument to the Miner, an imposing bronze sculpture, the work of Pepe Noja, which represents the mining history of the city, borders the volcanic area of Campo de Calatrava and in its vicinity is the Valley of Alcudia. Mining activity is of special importance with the Mining Museum, a building that surrounds an old headframe and simulates a coal selection workshop. On its two floors, the museum discourse addresses the transformation of Puertollano into an industrial city due to the discovery of coal.
The mining tradition of this town is very present in its different corners, such as the Castillete de Santa María, which welcomes its visitors at the entrance to the city. It is also worth seeing the Monument to the Miner, mentioned above and located on the Cerro de Santa Ana, from which we will obtain a fantastic panoramic view of the city and its surroundings.

This dam creates the Montorillo Reservoir which was built in 1950. It controls the Montoro River so local communities and businesses have a safe source of water year-round. It also creates a good habitat for different birds and fish. The area around the reservoir is rural and creates a great area for mountain biking.
